Facts.text: Pope Pontian becomes the first Pope to abdicate from the role, for St. Anterus
Battle at Mursa: Emperor Constantine II beats Emperor Magnentius, Magnentius retreats to Gaul
Roman usurper Procopius bribes two legions passing by Constantinople, and proclaims himself Roman emperor
Saint Wenceslas is murdered by his brother, Boleslaus I of Bohemia
The Duchy of Bohemia's Slavník dynasty members Spytimír, Pobraslav, Pořej, and Čáslav are murdered by Boleslaus II the Pious
Battle of Muhldorf: the Duchy of (Upper) Bavaria defeats Austrian forces
Guillaum de Grimoard elected as Pope Urban V
Cardinal Pedro de Luna of Aragon chosen (anti)pope Benedictus XIII
Christian I is crowned king of Denmark
Battle at Preveza: Ottoman fleet under Barbarossa beats Papal alliance off Northwestern Greece
Istanbul experiences its greatest fire of the century after a blaze that starts in a Jewish bakery goes on to destroy 36,000 buildings over eight days
Knights of Malta attack Ottoman convoy of ships that include Chief Black Eunuch and pilgrims for Mecca, killing or selling all into slavery, brings about the Cretan War a year later
Battle of Elba /Monte Christo: Dutch fleet under Johan van Galen defeats an English fleet
Venetians take Athens from the Turks
Divorce legalized in Maryland
Russian and Austrian armies occupy Berlin
VOC government bans importation of South-East Asian slaves into the Cape because they are considered dangerous (not observed)
Congress sends Constitution to state legislatures for their approval
Walker's Appeal, racial antislavery pamphlet, published in Boston
British Association expedition to Antarctica ships Erebus and Terror set sail from England, to attempt to locate magnetic South Pole
Coronation of Oscar I of Sweden-Norway as King of Sweden-Norway in Stockholm, Sweden
US Navy abolishes flogging as punishment
Donati's comet becomes 1st to be photographed
-30) Battle of Fort Harrison Virginia (Chaffin's Farm New Market Heights)
Toronto becomes capital of Ontario
Battle of Alcolea, causes Queen Isabella II of Spain to flee to France
Opelousas Massacre at St Landry Parish, Louisiana (200 blacks killed)
"3 Fingers" Ranald Mackenzie destroys Kwahadi-Commanche village, Texas, killing 23 men and taking 120 women and children prisoner
Sydney, Australia, inaugurates steam motor tram route
Yellow River or Huáng Hé floods in China, killing between 900,000 and 2 million people, one of the deadliest natural disasters in history

First General Conference on Weights and Measures (CGPM) defines the length of a meter as the distance between two lines on a standard bar of an alloy of platinum with ten percent iridium, measured at the melting point of ice
1st night football game played (Mansfield Pa)
Simon Marks & Tom Spencer open Penny Bazaar in Manchester
Transvaal mobilizes its army - leadup to Second Boer War
Guerrilla's assault unarmed US soldiers at breakfast in Balangiga, Philippines, 44 killed; the abandoned town is burned in retalliation
Woman arrested for smoking a cigarette in a car on 5th Avenue, NYC
US troops reoccupy Cuba, stay until 1909
Great Musi river flood kills 15,000 people in Hyderabad, India, destroying much of the city - a 200 year-old tamarind tree saves 150 lives
NY Highlanders get 13 walk & steal 15 bases (including then record 6 in one inning), beating Browns 18-12; each team committed 6 errors
SS Kichemaru disappears in storm off Japanese coast, 1,000 die
German forces move into Antwerp, Belgium (WWI)
Battle of Kut-el-Amara: British defeat Turks in Mesopotamia
Fastest major league game (51 mins), Giants beat Phillies 6-1
Dirk Fock appointed as governor-general of Dutch East Indies
Abyssinia (Ethiopia) leaves League of Nations
New York Yankees overwhelm Boston Red Sox 24-4
Radio Times 1st published
2 US Army planes end around-world flight, Seattle to Seattle, 57 stops
French government names General Serrail governor-general of Syria
General Plutarco Calles elected President of Mexico
Russia & Latvia treaty of neutrality signed
1st recording session in Nashville (Warmack's Gully Jumpers)
Juan de la Cierva makes 1st helicopter flight over English Channel
NY Yanks clinch pennant #6
UK passes the Dangerous Drugs Act outlawing cannabis.
Valentin Katayev's "Kvadratura Kruga" premieres in Moscow
1st CF interception return for a touchdown (Joe Hess-U of Alberta)
200,000 demonstrators demand declaration of war on Japan in Peking
Bachelor's Children debuts on CBS radio (at 9:45 am)
Clare Boothe's "Boys Goodbye" premieres in NYC
Dutch Prime Minister Hendrikus Colijn sends radio message "No war coming"
Homer in the Gloamin' - famous walk-off home run hit byGabby Hartnettof the Chicago Cubs against the Pittsburgh Pirates
Estonia accepts Soviet military bases
Final broadcast of The Fleischmann Hour was heard on radio
Michigan'sTom Harmonruns 72, 86 & 94 yard touchdowns
Nazi occupiers present "New Dutch Culture" in German
Phillies lose club record 111th game
Luftwaffe bombs Stalingrad
NY Americans NHL team folded
1st TV Musical comedy (The Boys from Boise)
Nazi forces begin killing civilians in the Italian village of Marzabotto
Canadian football's Calgary Bronks changes its name to Stampeders
Robert T Duncan appears as Tonio in "I Pagliacci"
WBAP-TV, (NBC affiliate) Fort Worth Texas, begins broadcasting
Indonesia becomes 60th member of UN
Norm Van Brocklin of Rams passes for NFL record 554 yards
"Bob & Ray Show" TV Variety last air on NBC
"Juvenile Jury" TV Children's last airs on NBC, moved to CBS
"Racket Squad" TV Crime Drama last airs on CBS
Dutch government proclaims 5% general pay increase on Jan 1, 1954
KOAT TV channel 7 in Albuquerque, NM (ABC/PBS) begins broadcasting
WITN TV channel 7 in Washington, NC (NBC) begins broadcasting
"Dollar A Second" last airs on NBC-TV
"Gisele MacKenzie Show" debuts on NBC-TV
"Honeycomb," by Jimmie F. Rodgers hits #1
France adopts constitution
Guinea votes for independence from France
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
USAF selects Thor over Jupiter rocket for mass production as ICBM's
"Hennesey" debuts on CBS-TV
NASA's Explorer 6 satellite reveals details of intense radiation of Van Allen belt around Earth
"Millionaire" last airs on CBS-TV
"My Heart Has a Mind of Its Own" by Connie Francis hits #1
"Queen For A Day" moves to ABC-TV
"Sunrise at Campobello" premieres at Palace theater
Ted Williamshits his final homer #521 (off Jack Fisher)
"Doctor Kildare" debuts on NBC-TV
Syria withdraws from United Arab Republic
USN Comdr Forrest S Petersen takes X-15 to 30,720 m
Walt Disney's movie "Grey Friars Bobby" premieres
"Brighter Day" last airs on CBS-TV daytime
"I'm Dickens, He's Fenster" debuts on ABC-TV
"Jan Murray Show" last airs on NBC-TV
"Verdict Is Yours," last airs on CBS-TV
Alouette (Canada), 1st Canadian satellite, launched
Paddington tram depot fire destroys 65 trams in Brisbane, Australia.
The last airing of "The Tonight Show" prior to Johnny Carson becoming host airs on NBC-TV
"King Leonardo" cartoon last airs on NBC-TV
"Little Deuce Coupe" single by The Beach Boys peaks at #15
"Sally, Go 'Round The Roses" by Jaynetts, a one-hit wonder from the Bronx, NYC, peaks at #2
"Shari Lewis Show" last airs on NBC-TV
"Surfer Joe" by Surfaris peaks at #62
Giuseppe Cantarella roller-skates record 25.78 mph (415 k) for 440 yds
Novelty song "Martian Hop" by The Ran-Dells, from Villas, New Jersey, peaks at #16
NYC DJ "Murray The K" plays The Beatles "She Loves You" on the radio
Suriname governor A Currie resigns
Jack McKay in X-15 reaches 90 km
Taal Volcano explodes on Luzon in the Philippines, killing an estimated 100 people
Whitney Museum of American Art opens its controversial Marcel Breuer designed new building in Manhattan, New York (since relocated)
Walter Washington elected 1st mayor of Washington, D.C.
Alberto Giolani of Italy roller skates record 23.133 miles in 1 hr
Atlanta Chiefs beat San Diego Toros 3-0 for NASL championship
Chuck Hixson (Southern Methodist) completes 37 of record 69 passes
Marisa Danisi of West Germany roller skates record 21.995 miles in 1 hr
The Beatles' single "Hey Jude" hits #1 and stays at #1 for 9 weeks
Minnesota vs Baltimore, gains 530 yards passing!
NFL Minnesota Vikings QB Joe Kapp passes for 7 touchdowns vs Baltimore Colts in 52-14 home win
SPD wins West German Parliament elections
Intrepid (US) beats Gretel II (Australia) in 22nd America's Cup
TV game show "Words & Music" debuts on NBC
Hungarian cardinal József Mindszenty after 15 years refuge in US Embassy in Budapest is allowed to leave the country
NY Times reports growing interest of white youth in black gospel music
UK passes the Misuse of Drugs Act banning the medicinal use of cannabis.
Canada defeats the USSR in the eighth and final game of the ice hockey Summit Series
Japan & Communist China agree to re-establish diplomatic relations
ITT Building in New York City bombed to protest ITT's involvement in the September 11 1973 coup d'état in Chile.
Palestinian Terrorists hijack Austrian train
"Ain't Nothing Like The Real Thing" single byAretha Franklinpeaks at #47
"Live It Up Part 1" by the Isley Brothers peaks at #52
"Papa Don't Take No Mess, Part 1" byJames Brownpeaks at #31
"Rock Me Gently" single by Canadian singer-songwriter Andy Kim peaks at #1
"Surfin' USA" by Beach Boys re-enters chart & peaks at #36
British guitarist Mick Ronson joins the rock band "Mott the Hoople"
California AngelNolan Ryan3rd no-hitter beats Minnesota Twins, 4-0
Bill authorizes admission of women to military academies
Oakland A'sVida Blue, Glenn Abbott, Paul Linblad & Rollie Fingers, no-hit California Angels, 5-0
The Spaghetti House siege, in which nine people were taken as hostages, takes place in London.
US Congress passes Toxic Substances Control Act
Israeli Knesset endorses Camp David accord
Syrians & Lebanese engage in heavy fighting in Lebanon
Australian Williams driver Alan Jones clinches his first Formula 1 World Drivers Championship by winning the Canadian Grand Prix at Île Notre-Dame Circuit
Jaromir Wagner is 1st to fly Atlantic standing on wing
Joseph Paul Franklin, avowed racist, sentenced to life imprisonment for killing 2 black joggers in Salt Lake City
1st reports appear of death from cyanide-laced Tylenol capsules
NASA launches Intelsat V satellite, no. 505
Pompey the Greatis assassinated on orders of King Ptolemy of Egypt after landing in Egypt
Battle at Tinchebrai: English King Henry I beats his brother Robert, Duke of Normandy in Normandy
ExplorerJuan Rodríguez Cabrillois the first European to sail into San Diego Bay, naming it San Miguel and claiming it for Spain
Battle at Chocim Dniester: KingSigismund IIIPolish-Lithuanian Commonwealth defeats an invading Ottoman Imperial army
American Revolution: Samuel Huntington is elected President of the Continental Congress, succeedingJohn Jay
Joachim Murats fleet sails from Corsica to Naples
Prussia lifts its ban onAdolf Hitlerspeaking in public
US recognizes the Chinese nationalist government ofChiang Kai-shek
Lou Gehrig's errorless streak ends at 885 consecutive games
"Judge Priest" film directed byJohn Ford, starringWill Rogersis released
FDRdedicates Bonneville Dam on Columbia River (Oregon)

German–Soviet Boundary and Friendship Treaty is signed byJoachim von RibbentropandVyacheslav Molotov; redraws German and Soviet spheres of influence in central Europe and transfers most of Lithuania to the USSR
Bud Brennan, a fan at Memorial Stadium, races out of stands and attempts to tackleTom Harmonat 3-yard line, Harmon easily evades
Ted Williamsended the baseball season with .406 batting avg
Film noir "Mildred Pierce" starringJoan Crawfordopens at Strand Theatre in NYC; wins Academy Award for Best Actress
"My Friend Irma" premieres, 1st of 16 films starringDean Martin&Jerry Lewis
New York'sAllie Reynolds' 2nd no-hitter of 1951, an 8-0 home victory over Boston Red Sox; Yankees clinch pennant #18
St. Louis Cardinals outfielderStan Musialmakes his only major league pitching appearance, throwing one pitch to Chicago Cubs Frank Baumholtz
"Johnny CarsonShow" TV Variety last airs on CBS-TV
RCA Records reportsElvis Presleysold over 10 million records
DutchQueen Julianaopens the Velser Tunnels under the North Sea Canal
Edward FranklinAlbee's play "Zoo Story" premieres in Berlin
"Hazel" starringShirley Boothdebuts on NBC-TV
American medical drama series "Dr. Kildare", starringRichard Chamberlainand Raymond Massey, premieres on NBC-TV
"NewPhil SilversShow" debuts on CBS-TV
"Nick Teen & Al K. Hall" byRolf Harrispeaks at #95
"Tennessee Tuxedo and His Tales" cartoon voiced byDon Adamsand Larry Storch debuts on CBS-TV
Janis Joplinannounces she's leaving the rock band "Big Brother & Holding Co"
Anwar Sadatreplaces the lateGamal Abdel Nasseras acting Egyptian President; elected president in October 1970
David Bowiesells out his 1st show at NYC's Carnegie Hall
CBS-TV airs singerCass Elliot's variety show special "Don't Call Me Mama Any More"; guests includeJoel Grey,Dick Van Dyke, and Bruno the Bear (aka Gentle Ben)
"I'm A Ramblin' Man" byWaylon Jenningspeaks at #75 on the pop chart, and #1 on the country chart
John Lennonappears as guest DJ on WNEW-FM (NYC)
US 1st LadyBetty Fordundergoes a radical mastectomy
A&M Records suesGeorge Harrisonwho fails to meet deadline for LP completion
Muhammad AlibeatsKen Nortonin a 15 round unanimous points decision for the WBA, WBC and The Ring heavyweight boxing titles

Tamla/Motown Records releases "Songs in the Key of Life", the 18th studio album byStevie Wonder; double album plus EP is biggest seller, includes "Sir Duke", "As", and "Isn't She Lovely" and wins Grammy for album of the year (1977)
Metal band Black Sabbath release their 8th album "Never Say Die", last to featureOzzy Osbournebefore he was fired
Larry Holmesbeats Earnie Shavers by TKO in 11th round in Las Vegas for WBC heavyweight boxing title
Carl Sagan's 13 part "Cosmos" premieres on PBS
Olivia Newton-Johnreleases single "Physical" (Billboard Song of the Year 1982)
William the Conqueror, then Duke of Normandy, lands at Pevensey Bay in Sussex, beginning the Norman Conquest of England
9,000 American and 7,000 French troops begin the Siege of Yorktown
Napoléon Bonaparte, aged 16, graduates from the elite École Militaire in Paris, ranking a lowly 42nd in a class of 51
Theodore Roosevelt Jr., son of President Theodore Roosevelt, is posthumously awarded the Medal of Honor for directing troops at Utah Beach during the D-Day landings

Henry Arthur Jones, English playwright (Judah), born in Grandborough, Buckinghamshire, England (d. 1929)
